<highlights>
<highlight index='1' description='Accurate statistical advice regarding log transformations.'>Since your residuals are not normally distributed due to outliers, you might consider transforming your dependent variable, like using a log transformation on the housing prices.</highlight>
<highlight index='2' description='Professional suggestion of robust regression techniques.'>Alternatively, you could use robust regression techniques, which are specifically designed to be less sensitive to the influence of outliers while keeping the model structure clear.</highlight>
<highlight index='3' description='Technical explanation of M-estimation and Huber regression.'>For your skewed distribution, M-estimation or Huber regression within a robust framework are good starting points, as they balance efficiency with resistance to outliers.</highlight>
<highlight index='4' description='Correct guidance on checking for heteroscedasticity.'>You absolutely should check for heteroscedasticity, as non-constant variance in residuals can make your standard errors unreliable, even if the distribution looks okay otherwise.</highlight>
</highlights>
